title: Proposal to add simd_invoke to std::simd
summary: Falls short on Q5 (no argument connecting the proposal to C++ language philosophy).

findings.question: Q5
findings.title: No argument connecting the proposal to C++ language philosophy
findings.gap: The paper does not argue how chunked_invoke aligns with any broader C++ design philosophy such as zero-overhead abstraction, composability, or trust-the-programmer.
findings.would_pass: A passing treatment would include an explicit argument that chunked_invoke upholds a recognized C++ design principle — e.g., that it provides zero-overhead abstraction over manual chunk/cat patterns, or that it preserves the language's trust-the-programmer ethos by letting users access platform intrinsics safely.
